title: Startup Operating Agreement (LLC)
description: A multi-member operating agreement for tech startups forming as LLCs, detailing member interests and voting majorities.
whatsCovered: LLC capital contributions and initial member splits
Voting classes, manager appointments, and majorities
Capital call requirements and dilution terms
Buy-sell rights and LLC dissolution clauses
whenToUse: Use this LLC operating agreement when forming a multi-member startup LLC to structure equity distributions, officer roles, and capital calls.

fields.label: Company Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 100 Innovators Blvd, San Francisco, CA

clauses.heading: STARTUP OPERATING AGREEMENT (LLC)
clauses.body: This startup agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {company_name}, located at {company_address} (the "Company"), and the counterparty or stakeholders executing this document below.

clauses.heading: 1. Core Agreement Terms
clauses.body: This agreement sets out the transaction and operational terms for Startup Operating Agreement (LLC). The parties agree to coordinate in good faith to complete milestones, share resources, or grant rights as specified.

clauses.heading: 2. Member Voting majorities
clauses.body: LLC {llc_name} has {member_count} initial members. Major decisions regarding sales or capital calls require a voting majority of {voting_threshold}%.

clauses.heading: 3. Proprietary Information and Secrecy
clauses.body: Both parties agree to treat all financial metrics, corporate plans, cap tables, and research files received during the execution of this Agreement as confidential and proprietary.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Why do we need an operating agreement?
faq.answer: It prevents internal disputes by contractually setting out voting rules, capital requirements, and what happens if a partner quits.

faq.question: Is this suitable for a Delaware LLC?
faq.answer: Yes, it contains standard provisions aligned with Delaware LLC law, which is the preferred choice for venture-backed startups.
